title: asynchronous preset/reset issue
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Use code examples from nReset_test.pdf then synthesize them. If you use Yosys 0.8 for synthesizing everything works as expected, whereas if you are using Yosys 0.9 you will get a message described in the Actual behavior part.  One code example is shown below:
```
module top (
    input nReset,
    input in,
    input clk,
    output reg q);
  
    always @(posedge clk or negedge nReset) 
    begin
        if(!nReset)
            q <= 1'b1;
        else
            q <= in;
    end
```


## Expected behavior

The message shown below describes the resource estimation and schematic corresponds to the code:

```
2.23. Printing statistics.
=== top ===
   Number of wires:                  4
   Number of wire bits:              4
   Number of public wires:           4
   Number of public wire bits:       4
   Number of memories:               0
   Number of memory bits:            0
   Number of processes:              0
   Number of cells:                  1
     FDPE                            1
2.24. Executing CHECK pass (checking for obvious problems).
checking module top..
found and reported 0 problems.
```

## Actual behavior

An error message that describes what the issue is:

```
2.22.3. Continuing TECHMAP pass.
Using template _90_dff_pn1_to_pp1 for cells of type $_DFF_PN1_.
<suppressed ~1 debug messages>
ERROR: Found error in internal cell \top.$auto$simplemap.cc:496:simplemap_adff$58 ($_DFF_PP1) at kernel/rtlil.cc:1307:
  attribute \module_not_derived 1
  attribute \src "test.v:8|/usr/bin/../share/yosys/xilinx/cells_map.v:28"
  cell $_DFF_PP1 $auto$simplemap.cc:496:simplemap_adff$58
    connect \R $techmap$techmap108$auto$simplemap.cc:496:simplemap_adff$58.$not$/usr/bin/../share/yosys/xilinx/cells_map.v:28$106_Y
    connect \C \clk
    connect \Q \q
    connect \D \in
  end
```
[nReset_test.pdf](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/6067620/nReset_test.pdf)
